skill.name: exa-search
skill.description: Semantic search, similar content discovery, and structured research using Exa API
skill.summary: AI-powered semantic search and research tool leveraging Exa API for embedding-based content discovery
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external_commands and network patterns, but all findings are false positives. The external_commands pattern is a standard npx package installation command. The network pattern is a documentation link to GitHub, not actual network code. No cryptographic code exists in the file. This is a documentation-only skill for Exa API integration.

low_findings.title: Standard Package Installation Command
low_findings.description: The skill uses npx to install packages, which is standard Node.js package management. This is expected behavior for CLI-based skills and poses no security risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx command is a legitimate npm package runner used for installing Node.js packages. No malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Documentation Link to External Repository
low_findings.description: Contains a reference link to the GitHub repository for examples. This is documentation only and does not execute any network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a markdown hyperlink for user reference, not executable code. No actual network calls are made from this file.

content.value_statement: Finding relevant information online is time-consuming with traditional keyword search. This skill enables semantic search using AI embeddings to discover similar content and conduct structured research through the Exa API.
actual_capabilities: Perform semantic search using AI embeddings to find conceptually similar content
Discover related documents and resources based on meaning rather than keywords
Search structured data categories including companies, people, and research papers
Integrate Exa API functionality directly into Claude Code conversations
Enable natural language queries for complex research tasks
limitations: Requires a valid Exa API key to function
Search results depend on Exa API index coverage and freshness
Rate limits apply based on your Exa API subscription tier
Cannot access private or authenticated web content

use_cases.title: Academic Research Discovery
use_cases.description: Find relevant research papers and academic resources by searching for concepts rather than exact keywords.

use_cases.title: Competitive Intelligence
use_cases.description: Discover company information and market data using structured category searches.

use_cases.title: Content Research
use_cases.description: Find similar articles and resources to expand knowledge on a specific topic.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Semantic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for content about [topic] using semantic search to find the most relevant results.

prompt_templates.title: Similar Content Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Find content similar to [URL or document] using embedding-based similarity search.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Category Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [type: companies/people/papers] related to [topic] using Exa category filtering.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Research Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct structured research on [topic] by performing semantic search, filtering by [criteria], and organizing results by [relevance/date/category].

output_examples.input: Find research papers about neural network optimization
output_examples.output: Returns a curated list of academic papers with titles, authors, publication dates, and relevance scores matching the semantic meaning of neural network optimization techniques.

output_examples.input: Find companies similar to the ones in this article about AI startups
output_examples.output: Provides a list of AI companies with descriptions, funding information, and similarity scores based on the embedding analysis of the reference content.

best_practices: Configure your Exa API key as an environment variable for secure access
Use specific and descriptive queries to improve semantic search relevance
Review API rate limits and plan batch operations accordingly
anti_patterns: Using overly broad or vague search queries that return unfocused results
Making rapid sequential API calls without respecting rate limits
Assuming semantic search returns exact keyword matches rather than conceptually similar content

faq.question: How do I get an Exa API key?
faq.answer: Visit the Exa website and create an account to obtain your API key. Free and paid tiers are available depending on your usage needs.

faq.question: What is the difference between semantic search and keyword search?
faq.answer: Semantic search uses AI embeddings to understand the meaning behind queries, finding conceptually similar content even without matching keywords. Keyword search only finds exact text matches.

faq.question: Can I search for specific types of content like research papers or companies?
faq.answer: Yes, Exa supports category filtering to search within specific content types including research papers, companies, people, and more.

faq.question: How are search results ranked?
faq.answer: Results are ranked by semantic similarity scores based on embedding distance. You can also sort by date or other metadata depending on the query type.

faq.question: What happens if I exceed my API rate limit?
faq.answer: Exa will return rate limit errors. The skill will surface these errors so you can retry after the rate limit window resets or upgrade your plan.

faq.question: Can this skill access my local files or private data?
faq.answer: No, this skill only interacts with the Exa API for public web content. It cannot access your local files or private databases unless you explicitly provide that content in your query.
